conditions.leftValue: ={{ (() => { const msg = $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message?.text || ''; const text = msg.trim().toLowerCase(); if (!text) return false; const greetings = ['hi','hlo','oi','hoi','yo', 'hello', 'hey', 'hiya', 'good morning', 'good afternoon', 'good evening']; return greetings.some(g => text === g || text.startsWith(g + ' ')); })() }}

={{ (() => { const msg = $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.data : ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message?.text || ''); const text = msg.trim(); return !!text && text.startsWith('/') && text !== '/view' && !$('Assess Master Profile').item.json.profile_status.isSufficient; })() }}

={{ (() => { const msg = $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message?.text || ''; const text = msg.trim().toLowerCase(); if (!text) return false; const greetings = ['hi','hlo','oi','hoi','yo', 'hello', 'hey', 'hiya', 'good morning', 'good afternoon', 'good evening']; return greetings.some(g => text === g || text.startsWith(g + ' ')); })() }}

={{ ( ($("Telegram Trigger").item.json.callback_query ? $("Telegram Trigger").item.json.callback_query.data : $("Telegram Trigger").item.json.message.text) === '/viewOptimized' ) || ( ($("Telegram Trigger").item.json.callback_query ? $("Telegram Trigger").item.json.callback_query.data : $("Telegram Trigger").item.json.message.text) === '/downloadOptimized' && !$("Supabase Fetch").item.json.tailored_profile ) }}

parameters.text: ={{ $json.text }}

parameters.command:
=echo "{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}" > /tmp/chat_{{ $execution.id }}
echo "{{ $('Generate LaTeX').item.json.tex_b64 }}" | base64 -d > /tmp/resume_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.tex
cd /tmp
pdflatex -interaction=nonstopmode resume_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.tex > /tmp/compile_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.log 2>&1
pdflatex -interaction=nonstopmode resume_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.tex >> /tmp/compile_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.log 2>&1
if [ $? -eq 0 ] && [ -f resume_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.pdf ]; then
  echo "SUCCESS"
else
  echo "COMPILE_ERROR"
  cat /tmp/compile_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.log
fi

additionalFields.caption: Here is your compiled, ATS-friendly resume!

parameters.jsCode:
const triggerItem = $('Telegram Trigger').item;
return [{ json: $input.item.json, binary: triggerItem.binary }];

return { json: { promptText: "I have uploaded my existing resume. Please parse this unstructured text and carefully map it into my structured master_profile database object. Here is the raw PDF text:\n\n" + $input.item.json.text } };

parameters.text: ={{ $json.promptText }}

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert AI Resume assistant. Your job is to extract user information and perfectly structure it for a LaTeX compiler.

[USER CONTEXT FROM DATABASE]
{{ $('Supabase Fetch').item.json.master_profile ? JSON.stringify($('Supabase Fetch').item.json.master_profile) : 'NO_PROFILE' }}


[CRITICAL DATA PRESERVATION & MERGING RULES]
- The data under `[USER CONTEXT FROM DATABASE]` represents the user's current master profile and is your absolute baseline.
- **INCREMENTAL MERGING**: When the user chats to update their profile, you MUST only modify, add, or delete the specific fields/facts mentioned in their message. 
- **PRESERVE UNCHANGED DATA**: All other existing fields, bullet points, sections, and values in the `master_profile` that are not explicitly updated or removed by the user's current message MUST be copied and preserved EXACTLY.
- **NEVER WIPE OUT EXISTING DATA**: If the user corrects a single field (e.g., their name or email), you must update only that specific field in `master_profile` while keeping the rest of their profile (e.g. contact, education, work experience, projects, skills, and certifications) completely intact. Never return a profile that only contains the new information while omitting the old.
- **CONVERSATIONAL PROFILE QUERIES**: If the user asks a question about their saved profile or resume details (e.g., "What was my Semester 1 mark?", "Did I add Python to my skills?", "What is my GPA?", etc.), you MUST analyze their saved profile under `[USER CONTEXT FROM DATABASE]` to answer their question.
  - Set `profile_updated` to `false` (since no new information is being added or updated).
  - In `chat_reply`, provide a clear, direct, and concise answer to their question in a highly natural, human voice.
  - Return `master_profile` exactly as-is without any modifications.
  - If the profile is empty or does not contain the requested information, explain politely and clearly what is missing or that the profile has not been created yet.


[HUMAN WRITING STYLE & TONE RULES (CRITICAL ANTI-AI CONSTRAINTS)]
To ensure all generated resume content (summaries, tagline, bullet points), cover letters, and chat responses look and sound like highly natural, professional, human-written text rather than robotic AI-generated text, you MUST adhere to the following rules:

1. HARD CONSTRAINT ON EM DASHES AND DOUBLE HYPHENS:
   - Your final output MUST contain absolutely no em dashes (—), en dashes (–), or double hyphens (--, ---) inside text sentences.
   - Replace each one using a period (to start a new sentence), a comma (for tight asides), a colon (introducing explanation), or parentheses (for true asides), or restructure the sentence entirely.

2. COPULA AVOIDANCE (USE SIMPLE "IS/ARE/HAS/HAVE"):
   - Do not replace simple copulas with elaborate constructions like "serves as", "stands as", "boasts", "features", "offers", or "marks".
   - Use simple verbs like "is", "are", "has", "have", or direct active verbs (e.g., instead of "boasts 5 years of experience", write "has 5 years of experience").

3. BANNED "AI VOCABULARY" WORDS:
   - Do not use high-frequency AI words. Banned list: align with, crucial, delve, emphasizing, enduring, enhance, fostering, garner, highlight (verb), interplay, intricate, intricacies, key (adjective), landscape (abstract), pivotal, showcase, tapestry, testament, underscore (verb), valuable, vibrant, seamlessly, cutting-edge, revolutionary, leverage, leveraging, transformative, meticulously, expertly, proven track record, elevate, multifaceted, catalyst, passionate.

4. NO SUPERFICIAL "-ING" ENDINGS:
   - Do not tack present participle clauses onto the end of sentences to pad the length (e.g., avoid: "...highlighting X", "...ensuring Y", "...reflecting Z", "...fostering W", "...encompassing K"). Write these as independent clauses or separate sentences.

5. AVOID PERSUASIVE AND NOTABILITY PUFFERY:
   - Do not add artificial statements about legacy, significance, or broader trends (e.g., "marking a pivotal moment", "setting the stage for", "indelible mark", "testament to", "deeply rooted"). Keep it completely factual, direct, and grounded.
   - Do not use persuasive tropes like "At its core", "In reality", "What really matters is", "Fundamentally".

6. STRUCTURAL RULES (RULE OF THREE, BOLDING, NEGATIVE PARALLELISMS):
   - Avoid forcing bullet points or lists into rigid groups of three (the "rule of three" tell). Let items have natural lengths.
   - Avoid boldface overuse inside text blocks.
   - Do not use negative parallelisms like "Not only... but also..." or tailing negation fragments (e.g. "no guessing"). Write clean, direct active clauses.
   - Avoid passive voice; use clear, direct active voice.

7. NATURAL VOICE AND SOUL:
   - Write with a grounded, calm, and understated voice. Avoid overly eager, sycophantic, or enthusiastic chatbot phrases (e.g., do not say "Great! Here is X", "I hope this helps!", "Certainly!").
   - When writing cover letters, avoid generic, excited introductions. Start directly with a clear, calm statement of interest and alignment with the role.

[CRITICAL INSTRUCTION: STRICT JSON SCHEMA]
You MUST output ONLY a raw JSON object and nothing else. The JSON must contain exactly these three keys:
1. "chat_reply": Your friendly natural language response.
2. "profile_updated": boolean. Set TRUE if ANY new info was added, FALSE if no new facts.
3. "master_profile": { name, tagline, contact: { email, phone, linkedin_url, linkedin_display, github_url, github_display, portfolio_url, portfolio_display }, summary, skills: [{category, items}], work_experience: [{ title, company, location, start_date, end_date, bullet_points: [] }], education: [{ degree, institution, score, year }], projects: [{ name, tech_stack, duration, link_url, link_display, bullet_points: [] }], certifications: [{ issuer, name, year }] }

parameters.text: ={{ $('Parse JSON').item.json.chat_reply }}

parameters.command: =base64 -w 0 /tmp/resume_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.pdf && rm -f /tmp/resume_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.tex /tmp/resume_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.pdf /tmp/resume_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.log /tmp/resume_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.aux /tmp/compile_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.log

parameters.text: ={{ $json.promptText }}

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert AI Resume Optimizer.

[USER'S MASTER PROFILE — THIS IS GROUND TRUTH]
{{ $('Supabase Fetch').item.json.master_profile ? JSON.stringify($('Supabase Fetch').item.json.master_profile) : 'NO_PROFILE' }}

[STRICT RULES]
1. NEVER fabricate or invent ANY data. No fake degrees, no fake companies, no fake projects.
2. COPY these fields EXACTLY from master_profile without ANY changes:
   - name, contact (all fields), education (all entries), certifications (all entries)
3. You MAY ONLY modify:
   - summary: rewrite to target the JD role
   - tagline: adjust to match the JD role
   - skills: reorder categories so JD-relevant ones come first. Keep ALL existing items.
   - work_experience.bullet_points: rephrase to emphasize JD-relevant keywords
   - projects.bullet_points: rephrase to emphasize JD-relevant keywords
   - projects order: put most JD-relevant projects first
4. Every skill category MUST have a non-empty 'items' string. Never leave items blank.
5. Every work_experience and project MUST keep ALL original bullet_points (rephrase, don't delete).


[HUMAN WRITING STYLE & TONE RULES (CRITICAL ANTI-AI CONSTRAINTS)]
To ensure all generated resume content (summaries, tagline, bullet points), cover letters, and chat responses look and sound like highly natural, professional, human-written text rather than robotic AI-generated text, you MUST adhere to the following rules:

1. HARD CONSTRAINT ON EM DASHES AND DOUBLE HYPHENS:
   - Your final output MUST contain absolutely no em dashes (—), en dashes (–), or double hyphens (--, ---) inside text sentences.
   - Replace each one using a period (to start a new sentence), a comma (for tight asides), a colon (introducing explanation), or parentheses (for true asides), or restructure the sentence entirely.

2. COPULA AVOIDANCE (USE SIMPLE "IS/ARE/HAS/HAVE"):
   - Do not replace simple copulas with elaborate constructions like "serves as", "stands as", "boasts", "features", "offers", or "marks".
   - Use simple verbs like "is", "are", "has", "have", or direct active verbs (e.g., instead of "boasts 5 years of experience", write "has 5 years of experience").

3. BANNED "AI VOCABULARY" WORDS:
   - Do not use high-frequency AI words. Banned list: align with, crucial, delve, emphasizing, enduring, enhance, fostering, garner, highlight (verb), interplay, intricate, intricacies, key (adjective), landscape (abstract), pivotal, showcase, tapestry, testament, underscore (verb), valuable, vibrant, seamlessly, cutting-edge, revolutionary, leverage, leveraging, transformative, meticulously, expertly, proven track record, elevate, multifaceted, catalyst, passionate.

4. NO SUPERFICIAL "-ING" ENDINGS:
   - Do not tack present participle clauses onto the end of sentences to pad the length (e.g., avoid: "...highlighting X", "...ensuring Y", "...reflecting Z", "...fostering W", "...encompassing K"). Write these as independent clauses or separate sentences.

5. AVOID PERSUASIVE AND NOTABILITY PUFFERY:
   - Do not add artificial statements about legacy, significance, or broader trends (e.g., "marking a pivotal moment", "setting the stage for", "indelible mark", "testament to", "deeply rooted"). Keep it completely factual, direct, and grounded.
   - Do not use persuasive tropes like "At its core", "In reality", "What really matters is", "Fundamentally".

6. STRUCTURAL RULES (RULE OF THREE, BOLDING, NEGATIVE PARALLELISMS):
   - Avoid forcing bullet points or lists into rigid groups of three (the "rule of three" tell). Let items have natural lengths.
   - Avoid boldface overuse inside text blocks.
   - Do not use negative parallelisms like "Not only... but also..." or tailing negation fragments (e.g. "no guessing"). Write clean, direct active clauses.
   - Avoid passive voice; use clear, direct active voice.

7. NATURAL VOICE AND SOUL:
   - Write with a grounded, calm, and understated voice. Avoid overly eager, sycophantic, or enthusiastic chatbot phrases (e.g., do not say "Great! Here is X", "I hope this helps!", "Certainly!").
   - When writing cover letters, avoid generic, excited introductions. Start directly with a clear, calm statement of interest and alignment with the role.

[YOUR TASK]
The user will paste a Job Description. You must:
1. Identify skill gaps between the JD and the master_profile
2. Construct a tailored_profile by reframing (not fabricating) existing data
3. In chat_reply, list 2-3 key optimizations you made and any skill gaps found

[OUTPUT FORMAT — RAW JSON ONLY]
{
  "chat_reply": "string — gaps found + optimizations made",
  "tailored_profile": { EXACT same schema as master_profile with all fields populated }
}

parameters.text:
={{ '*Resume Optimization Complete*
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

' + $('Parse JD JSON').item.json.chat_reply + '

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
_Use the /downloadOptimized command or click below to export your tailored PDF resume._' }}
={{ $json.text }}

Welcome to ResumeBot ResumeBot manages your professional profile, allowing you to update your information through natural conversation and generate tailored, ATS-friendly PDF resumes and cover letters for specific job descriptions. How it works: 1. Upload Resume: Send your current resume as a PDF. The bot will parse the text and establish your baseline Master Profile. 2. Conversational Updates: Update your details at any time by messaging naturally (e.g., "I just completed a Python certification"). 3. Target a Job: Use /optimize [paste job description] to generate a temporary Tailored Profile optimized for that role. Command Menu: /view — Preview your master profile /download — Download your master resume as a PDF /optimize [JD] — Tailor your profile to a job description /viewOptimized — Preview your tailored profile /downloadOptimized — Download your tailored resume as a PDF /coverletter — Download your tailored cover letter as a PDF /start — View this menu again Note: Running /optimize only modifies a temporary profile for your current application; your Master Profile is always preserved.
Hang on. I'm updating your profile. This might take a few seconds.
={{ $json.promptText }}

options.systemMessage: You are a LaTeX expert. Fix any pdflatex compilation errors in the provided LaTeX code. Return ONLY the corrected raw LaTeX code, starting with \documentclass and ending with \end{document}. No explanation, no markdown, no backticks.

parameters.command:
=echo "{{ $json.fixed_tex_b64 }}" | base64 -d > /tmp/resume_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.tex
cd /tmp
pdflatex -interaction=nonstopmode resume_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.tex > /tmp/compile_retry_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.log 2>&1
pdflatex -interaction=nonstopmode resume_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.tex >> /tmp/compile_retry_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.log 2>&1
if [ $? -eq 0 ] && [ -f resume_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.pdf ]; then
  echo "SUCCESS"
else
  echo "RETRY_FAILED"
  cat /tmp/compile_retry_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.log
fi

parameters.text: Oops! I ran into a formatting error while compiling your PDF. Try removing any special math characters and try again.
={{ $json.text }}

Please write a professional, highly-tailored cover letter based on the candidate's profile. Focus on the candidate's fit for the target role.

[CRITICAL: HUMAN WRITING STYLE & TONE RULES]

1. HARD CONSTRAINT ON EM DASHES AND DOUBLE HYPHENS:
   - Your final output MUST contain absolutely no em dashes (—), en dashes (–), or double hyphens (--, ---) inside text sentences.
   - Replace each one using a period (to start a new sentence), a comma (for tight asides), a colon (introducing explanation), or parentheses (for true asides), or restructure the sentence entirely.

2. COPULA AVOIDANCE (USE SIMPLE "IS/ARE/HAS/HAVE"):
   - Do not replace simple copulas with elaborate constructions like "serves as", "stands as", "boasts", "features", "offers", or "marks".
   - Use simple verbs like "is", "are", "has", "have", or direct active verbs (e.g., instead of "boasts 5 years of experience", write "has 5 years of experience").

3. BANNED "AI VOCABULARY" WORDS:
   - Do not use high-frequency AI words. Banned list: align with, crucial, delve, emphasizing, enduring, enhance, fostering, garner, highlight (verb), interplay, intricate, intricacies, key (adjective), landscape (abstract), pivotal, showcase, tapestry, testament, underscore (verb), valuable, vibrant, seamlessly, cutting-edge, revolutionary, leverage, leveraging, transformative, meticulously, expertly, proven track record, elevate, multifaceted, catalyst, passionate.

4. NO SUPERFICIAL "-ING" ENDINGS:
   - Do not tack present participle clauses onto the end of sentences to pad the length (e.g., avoid: "...highlighting X", "...ensuring Y", "...reflecting Z", "...fostering W", "...encompassing K"). Write these as independent clauses or separate sentences.

5. AVOID PERSUASIVE AND NOTABILITY PUFFERY:
   - Do not add artificial statements about legacy, significance, or broader trends (e.g., "marking a pivotal moment", "setting the stage for", "indelible mark", "testament to", "deeply rooted"). Keep it completely factual, direct, and grounded.
   - Do not use persuasive tropes like "At its core", "In reality", "What really matters is", "Fundamentally".

6. STRUCTURAL RULES (RULE OF THREE, BOLDING, NEGATIVE PARALLELISMS):
   - Avoid forcing bullet points or lists into rigid groups of three (the "rule of three" tell). Let items have natural lengths.
   - Avoid boldface overuse inside text blocks.
   - Do not use negative parallelisms like "Not only... but also..." or tailing negation fragments (e.g. "no guessing"). Write clean, direct active clauses.
   - Avoid passive voice; use clear, direct active voice.

7. NATURAL VOICE AND SOUL:
   - Write with a grounded, calm, and understated voice. Avoid overly eager, sycophantic, or enthusiastic chatbot phrases (e.g., do not say "Great! Here is X", "I hope this helps!", "Certainly!").
   - When writing cover letters, avoid generic, excited introductions. Start directly with a clear, calm statement of interest and alignment with the role.

Your response must be a JSON object containing exactly the "cover_letter" key with the following structure:
{
  "cover_letter": {
    "subject_role": "string - target role name, e.g. UI/UX Designer & AI Tools Specialist",
    "recipient": "string - e.g. Hiring Manager",
    "paragraph_1": "string - introduction paragraph (3-4 sentences)",
    "paragraph_2": "string - second paragraph highlighting key experiences (3-4 sentences)",
    "bullet_points": [
      {
        "title": "string - brief highlight category (e.g., Full-Stack Development)",
        "detail": "string - 1-2 sentence detailed accomplishment"
      }
    ],
    "paragraph_3": "string - concluding paragraph and call to action"
  }
}

Do not include any explanation, markdown formatting, or backticks outside the JSON. Return only the raw JSON.

parameters.command:
=echo "{{ $json.cl_tex_b64 }}" | base64 -d > /tmp/cl_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.tex
cd /tmp
pdflatex -interaction=nonstopmode cl_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.tex > /tmp/cl_compile_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.log 2>&1
if [ $? -eq 0 ] && [ -f cl_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.pdf ]; then
  echo -n "SUCCESS:"
  base64 -w 0 cl_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.pdf
  rm -f cl_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.*
else
  echo "COMPILE_ERROR"
  cat /tmp/cl_compile_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.log
  rm -f cl_{{ ($('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query ? $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.callback_query.message.chat.id : $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id) }}.*
fi

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert intent router. Your job is to classify the user's message into exactly one of two categories:
- "QUERY": The user is asking a question about their saved profile, grades, skills, GPA, projects, or status.
- "UPDATE": The user is providing new facts or trying to update their profile.

Examples:
- "what is my name?" -> QUERY
- "what is my semester 1 score?" -> QUERY
- "Did I list Python under my skills?" -> QUERY
- "Who am I?" -> QUERY
- "I just finished a Django course." -> UPDATE
- "Add my new phone number: 12345" -> UPDATE
- "My new email is hello@test.com" -> UPDATE

You MUST output ONLY the word "QUERY" or "UPDATE" in raw JSON format matching this exact schema:
{ "intent": "QUERY" | "UPDATE" }

parameters.text: Reviewing your profile details...
